preamble: Deaffrication / Spirantization. The voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] loses its stop onset, becoming the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ]. This is a common historical shift in several Romance languages.

examples.note: Historical development where the affricate [dʒ] became the fricative [ʒ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
